steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Left side: “...but many are still well” — an independent clause needing an adjective to complete it. Right side: “Malika Ouattara, for example, is a celebrated slam poet from Burkina Faso.” — a complete, independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A colon separates two independent clauses when the second elaborates on or exemplifies the first. “Known:” introduces Malika Ouattara as an example of a writer of other literary forms who is still well-known.

The other choices fail to properly separate two independent clauses — either leaving them unseparated, joining them incorrectly with only a comma, or creating an ungrammatical construction.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
